01 /

About

I'm a mechanical engineer who lives on the quality and process side of manufacturing, where the goal is easy to state and hard to deliver: make a production line predictable, capable, and defect-free.

I hold a Master's in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Alabama at Birmingham (GPA 3.77) and have spent 2+ years on Tier-1 automotive lines, working hands-on with CNC machining, dimensional inspection, PFMEA, control plans, and root-cause and corrective-action systems.

What ties it together is the mix of floor-level instinct and graduate training in automated manufacturing, quality and statistics, and Lean Six Sigma. I read a process, isolate the variation that matters, and engineer it out with data rather than guesswork. I'm now looking to bring that blend of hands-on manufacturing and structured problem-solving to a Quality, Manufacturing, or Process Engineering role with a U.S. manufacturer.

04 /

Selected Projects

UAB · QUALITY & STATISTICS · 2024

Process Capability & SPC Study

A 30-sample dimensional study on a machined length, completed in Minitab for graduate Quality & Statistics coursework. I tested the data for normality with an Anderson-Darling test (p = 0.098, normal), checked statistical control with individuals and moving-range charts, and computed process-capability indices (Cp, Cpk, Pp, Ppk) against specification limits. It follows the same measure, visualize, test, and quantify workflow used in a real SPC or PPAP capability submission.

UAB · AUTOMATED MANUFACTURING · 2025 · TEAM OF 4

Peaucellier–Lipkin Straight-Line Linkage

A four-person build of a Peaucellier–Lipkin mechanism, which converts a motor's circular motion into true linear motion (six links, five joints, rhombus geometry). We started with no supplied dimensions and developed our own geometry and tolerancing, sizing every link, pin and the base by hand. To survive 3D printing we set a 0.05 mm clearance on all holes and seated a 22 mm bearing in a 22.05 mm pocket so the parts fit cleanly after printing.

I modeled the full assembly in SolidWorks, ran a motion study to confirm the kinematics, and validated the structure in ANSYS. We printed the parts in different plastics and sizes to tune fit and strength, assembled them into a working unit, and automated it with a stepper motor driven by an Arduino, so the linkage runs on its own and traces a clean straight line.

B.TECH · 2019–2020 · RUNNING PROTOTYPE

Electric Bike: Design & Fabrication

Built a working electric bike from the ground up. I worked through the full cycle: frame and chassis design, mechanical fabrication, motor selection and mounting, and battery-pack design and integration, then assembled and tested a complete running prototype. The finished bike reached a top speed of around 60 km/h, with a hub motor driving the rear wheel and a custom multi-battery pack feeding the drivetrain.

TEAM LEAD · 2023

Agricultural Robot

Led a team to design and build an agricultural robot for field tasks. Modeled the mechanical structure in CATIA, integrated Arduino-based controls and actuators, and iterated through functional testing and troubleshooting to improve reliability, simplify assembly, and cut operating effort for the end user.

B.TECH · 2021

Power Plant Layout

Designed the facility layout for a power plant: equipment placement, material and utility flow, and operator access, using facility-layout and material-handling principles to cut travel and avoid bottlenecks. We then built a physical scale prototype of the layout to validate the arrangement and demonstrate plant flow.
